description: WHATWG-normative `text/event-stream` byte traces paired with the decoded events a conformant decoder MUST produce. Each trace exercises one normative rule so the conformance plan can assert each rule independently.

single-data-default-type.description: A single `data:` field followed by an empty line dispatches one event whose `type` defaults to `message`.

multi-data-lf-concat.description: Multiple `data:` fields in one frame are concatenated with a single LF between them; the trailing LF that the algorithm appends is stripped before dispatch.

crlf-line-terminator.description: CRLF line endings are valid frame separators alongside LF and CR.

cr-line-terminator.description: Bare CR line endings are valid frame separators per WHATWG.

named-event-type.description: An `event:` field sets the dispatched event's `type` to that value.

id-sets-last-event-id.description: An `id:` field sets the last-event-id buffer which the decoder MUST surface on dispatch and which the client MUST send via `Last-Event-ID` on reconnection.

id-with-null-byte-ignored.description: An `id:` field whose value contains U+0000 NULL MUST be ignored: the last-event-id buffer remains unchanged.
id-with-null-byte-ignored.encodedBytes:
id: legit
data: keep

id: bad�value
data: still-legit

id-empty-resets-last-event-id.description: Per WHATWG `https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#event-stream-interpretation`, an `id:` field with an empty value sets the last-event-id buffer to the empty string, which suppresses the `Last-Event-ID` header on reconnect. The reset MUST be observable on subsequent dispatched events and on the surfaced `lastEventId`.
id-empty-resets-last-event-id.encodedBytes:
id: 42
data: first

id:
data: second

retry-digits-only.description: A `retry:` field whose value is ASCII digits sets the reconnect delay; non-digit `retry:` values MUST be ignored.
retry-digits-only.encodedBytes:
retry: 5000
data: with-retry

retry: not-a-number
data: ignored-retry

comment-line-ignored.description: A line that starts with U+003A COLON is a comment per WHATWG and MUST NOT surface as a decoded event; it MAY serve as a keep-alive.

leading-space-stripped-once.description: If a field value starts with U+0020 SPACE, the decoder strips ONE leading space. Subsequent spaces are preserved.

field-without-value.description: A line with no U+003A COLON is treated as a field with an empty value. `data` (no value, no colon) appends an empty string then a LF.

empty-data-buffer-no-dispatch.description: An empty line dispatches an event only when the data buffer is non-empty. A frame consisting solely of comment + empty line MUST NOT dispatch.

leading-bom-stripped.description: The UTF-8 decode step strips at most one leading U+FEFF BYTE ORDER MARK from the stream.

event-type-resets-between-frames.description: The event-type buffer is reset between dispatched frames: an `event:` field on one frame does NOT carry over to the next frame.
event-type-resets-between-frames.encodedBytes:
event: typed
data: first

data: second

multiple-frames-ordered.description: Decoded frames MUST be emitted in the order they appear in the byte trace; ordering is observable evidence.
multiple-frames-ordered.encodedBytes:
data: a

data: b

data: c

id-persists-until-next-id.description: The last-event-id buffer persists across events that do not redeclare `id:`. Each subsequent decoded event surfaces the last value seen.
id-persists-until-next-id.encodedBytes:
id: a1
data: one

data: two

id: a2
data: three

unterminated-final-frame-no-dispatch.description: Per WHATWG: at end-of-stream any pending data must be discarded; an incomplete frame that lacks the terminating empty line MUST NOT dispatch. A conformant decoder dispatches only the frames that were closed with a blank-line transition and drops the trailing partial frame.
